properties.name: autoPlay
properties.description: v1 prop name: autoPlay (camelCase).

properties.name: childKeys
properties.description: v1: child Text component is rendered inside the button via childKeys.

properties.name: childKeys
properties.description: v1: a single child key, delivered via childKeys[0] from the render framework.

properties.name: enableDate
properties.description: v1 prop: enableDate — include date portion.

properties.name: enableTime
properties.description: v1 prop: enableTime — include time portion.

properties.name: htmlInputType
properties.description: Derives HTML input type from enableDate + enableTime.

properties.name: value
properties.description: v1 prop: value (resolved DynamicString).

properties.name: icon
properties.description: v1 prop name: icon (resolved string, e.g. a Unicode symbol or ligature name).

properties.name: listClass
properties.type: Signal<"flex flex-row gap-1 overflow-x-auto" | "flex flex-col gap-1 overflow-y-auto max-h-96">

properties.name: childKeys
properties.description:
v1: childKeys[0] = entryPointChild (inline trigger),
    childKeys[1] = contentChild (modal body).

properties.name: title
properties.description: Resolved title string (from optional title DynamicString).

properties.name: maxAllowedSelections
properties.description: When ≤ 1 — render as single-select <select>; otherwise multi-select checkboxes.

properties.name: options
properties.description: Resolved options with plain string labels (surface-to-spec resolves DynamicString).

properties.name: selections
properties.description: Resolved current selections (plain string array from surface-to-spec).

properties.name: maxValue
properties.description: v1 prop: maxValue.

properties.name: minValue
properties.description: v1 prop: minValue.

properties.name: value
properties.description: v1 prop: value (resolved DynamicNumber).

properties.name: internalHandlers
properties.description: Merge built-in A2UI handlers with consumer-provided handlers.

properties.name: registry
properties.description: Convert ViewRegistry to AngularRegistry for RenderSpecComponent.

properties.name: spec
properties.description: Convert the A2UI surface to a json-render Spec for rendering.

properties.name: childKeys
properties.description: v1: each child key corresponds to a tab's contentChild (childKeys[i] ↔ tabTitles[i]).

properties.name: tabTitles
properties.description: Resolved tab titles from tabItems[*].title — produced by surface-to-spec.

properties.name: text
properties.description: v1 prop: text (resolved string value).

properties.name: value
properties.description: Back-compat alias: value. surface-to-spec resolves DynamicString → plain string.

properties.name: autoPlay
properties.description: v1 prop name: autoPlay (camelCase).

name: ChatCitationCardTemplateDirective
description:
ContentChild template directive for custom citation card rendering.
Usage: <ng-template chatCitationCard let-citation>...</ng-template>

properties.name: citations
properties.description:
Combined citation list:
  1. Message.citations (provider-populated, takes precedence by id)
  2. Markdown sidecar defs (Pandoc-formatted [^id]: lines), merged in
     for any id not already present.

Sorted by index ascending. This guarantees the sources panel surfaces
citations whether they come from message metadata, content syntax, or
both — matching the same precedence as inline-marker resolution.

properties.name: messageCopy
properties.description: Emitted when the user copies an assistant message.

properties.name: modelOptions
properties.description:
High-level model-picker API. When `modelOptions` is non-empty, the chat
composition renders a `<chat-select>` inside the input pill (in BOTH
welcome and conversation modes), wired to the two-way `selectedModel`
model. Consumers who want full control should leave `modelOptions`
empty and project a `<chat-select chatInputModelSelect>` themselves.

properties.name: rate
properties.description: Emitted when the user rates an assistant message.

properties.name: regenerate
properties.description: Emitted when the user clicks the regenerate button on an assistant message.

methods.name: isReasoningStreaming
methods.description:
True while a message's reasoning is mid-stream — i.e. it's the latest
message, the agent is loading, the message has reasoning content, and
no response text has arrived yet. Once the response text begins, the
reasoning pill collapses (per its internal logic).

methods.name: onA2uiEvent
methods.signature: onA2uiEvent(event: RenderEvent, messageIndex: number, surfaceId: string)

methods.name: onRegenerate
methods.description: Regenerate the assistant response at the given message index.

properties.name: canStop
properties.description: The stop button only appears when the consumer opted in AND we're loading.

properties.name: canSubmit
properties.description: Submit is allowed only when not loading and there's non-whitespace text.

properties.name: composing
properties.description: True while an IME composition (CJK input, accent, autocorrect) is active.

properties.name: showStopButton
properties.description: When true (default), shows a stop button while the agent is streaming.

methods.name: onStop
methods.description: Abort the current streaming response (if the adapter supports it).

name: ChatMessageActionsComponent
description:
Default action buttons that appear under each assistant message:
regenerate, copy-to-clipboard, thumbs up, thumbs down.

Hidden by default, fades in on `:hover`/`:focus-within` of the parent
`chat-message`, and stays visible on the current/last assistant message
and on mobile.

properties.name: content
properties.description: Plain text content to copy. Required for the copy button to function.

properties.name: contentCopied
properties.description: Emitted with the copied content after a successful clipboard write.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: When true, the regenerate button is disabled (e.g. while the agent is streaming).

properties.name: rate
properties.description: Emitted with 'up' or 'down' when the user rates the response.

properties.name: regenerate
properties.description: Emitted when the user clicks regenerate. Wire this to `agent.regenerate(index)`.

properties.name: closeOnEscape
properties.description: Close the popup on Escape (default true).

properties.name: shortcut
properties.description:
Keyboard shortcut (single key) that toggles the popup with cmd (mac)
or ctrl (other). Set to `null` to disable. Default: 'k' — matches the
widely-used cmd/ctrl+K convention.

properties.name: views
properties.description:
A2UI component catalog forwarded to the inner <chat>. Without it,
messages classified as A2UI parse correctly but never mount a
surface. Pass `a2uiBasicCatalog()` from `@ngaf/chat`.

name: ChatReasoningComponent
description:
Renders an assistant's reasoning content as a compact pill that
expands to reveal the underlying text. Three visual states:

- Streaming: pill shows "Thinking…" with a pulsing dot; auto-expanded
  so the user sees reasoning stream in real time.
- Idle, with durationMs known: pill shows "Thought for {duration}";
  collapsed by default, expand on click.
- Idle, no duration: pill shows "Show reasoning"; collapsed by default.

The body re-uses chat-streaming-md so reasoning content gets the same
markdown rendering pipeline as the visible response (lists, code,
step labels often appear in reasoning output).

Internal state: a tristate "expanded" — null means follow auto state-
driven logic (force-expand on isStreaming, otherwise honor
defaultExpanded), boolean is a manual user choice that wins for the
lifetime of the instance.

name: ChatSelectComponent
description:
Generic single-select dropdown. Designed to slot into the chat input pill
(via [chatInputModelSelect]) but usable anywhere.

Inputs:
  options      — array of { value, label, disabled? }; required
  value        — currently selected value (two-way via model())
  placeholder  — trigger label when no option matches; default 'Select'
  disabled     — disables the trigger; default false
  menuLabel    — aria-label for the popover; defaults to placeholder

properties.name: views
properties.description:
A2UI component catalog forwarded to the inner <chat>. Without it,
messages classified as A2UI parse correctly but never mount a
surface. Pass `a2uiBasicCatalog()` from `@ngaf/chat`.

name: ChatStreamingMdComponent
description:
Renders streaming markdown by walking a @cacheplane/partial-markdown AST
through @ngaf/render's view registry.

Reactivity model: the live `parser.root` keeps a stable JS reference
across pushes (partial-markdown's identity guarantee). To make Angular
signals propagate downstream when the underlying tree changes, we surface
a materialized snapshot via `materialize()`. The snapshot shares
structurally — unchanged subtrees keep the SAME reference, and any
descendant change yields a NEW root reference. This lets Angular's
`Object.is` equality check both detect changes (root reference differs)
and short-circuit unchanged subtrees (per-node references stable).

Override per-node-type renderers via the `[viewRegistry]` input or by
supplying a different `MARKDOWN_VIEW_REGISTRY` provider in the injector
tree.

properties.name: templates
properties.description: Per-tool-name + wildcard templates registered as content children.

name: ChatToolCallTemplateDirective
description:
Registers a per-tool-name template inside <chat-tool-calls>. The
primitive collects all directive instances via contentChildren() and
dispatches incoming calls by their `name` field. A literal "*" name
registers a wildcard catch-all that handles any tool name without a
specific template registered.

Usage:

  <chat-tool-calls [agent]="agent" [message]="msg">
    <ng-template chatToolCallTemplate="search_web" let-call let-status="status">
      <my-search-result-card [query]="call.args.query" [status]="status"/>
    </ng-template>
    <ng-template chatToolCallTemplate="*" let-call>
      <chat-tool-call-card [toolCall]="call"/>
    </ng-template>
  </chat-tool-calls>

properties.name: name
properties.description: The tool name this template handles, or "*" for the wildcard catch-all.

properties.name: defaultExpanded
properties.description: When state is not 'running' or 'error', honors this input as the default expansion.

name: ChatWelcomeComponent
description:
Empty-state owner. Renders a centered greeting + slot-projected input +
optional vertical suggestion rows. Mounted only when the parent chat has
no messages and welcome is not disabled.

Slots:
  [chatWelcomeTitle]       — replaces the default <h1> "How can I help?"
  [chatWelcomeInput]       — projects the chat input into the center column
  [chatWelcomeSuggestions] — projects suggestion rows below the input

Host CSS variables (override on :host or any ancestor):
  --ngaf-chat-welcome-max-width  default 36rem
  --ngaf-chat-welcome-gap        default 1.25rem
  --ngaf-chat-welcome-padding    default 24px

name: MarkdownChildrenComponent
description:
Recursively dispatches a parent node's children through the markdown view
registry. Each child's `type` is looked up in the registry; the resolved
component is rendered with `[node]` bound to that child.

Identity-preserving: `track $any(child)` keys on the JS reference of the
child node. Because @cacheplane/partial-markdown preserves node identity
across pushes, unchanged subtrees never re-render.

name: Agent
description:
Runtime-neutral contract chat primitives consume.

Implementations are produced by runtime adapters (e.g. a LangGraph or
AG-UI adapter) or by user code for custom backends.

`interrupt` and `subagents` are optional: runtimes that do not support these
concepts should leave them undefined, and primitives that need them check
presence and render a neutral fallback when absent.

Invariant: state lives on signals; `events$` carries only things that are
not derivable from signals.

properties.name: regenerate
properties.description:
Discards the assistant message at the given index AND all messages after
it, then re-runs the agent against the trimmed conversation tail. The
preceding user message (at index - 1) is preserved and re-submitted as
the agent's input. No new user message is added to the history.

Throws if the message at `index` is not 'assistant' role, or if the
agent is currently loading another response.

name: AgentCheckpoint
description:
Runtime-neutral snapshot of a point in an agent's execution history.

Consumed by time-travel / debug UIs. `id` is adapter-opaque — UIs emit
it back to the parent app on replay/fork, and the parent app dispatches
to the underlying runtime.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Adapter-opaque checkpoint identifier (e.g. LangGraph checkpoint_id).

properties.name: label
properties.description: Human-friendly label for the checkpoint (e.g. next node name).

properties.name: values
properties.description: State snapshot at this checkpoint.

name: AgentCustomEvent
description:
Escape hatch for runtime-specific or user-defined events that do not
(yet) have a well-known structured variant. `name` carries the runtime
event name; `data` carries the payload verbatim.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Stable identifier for this interrupt instance.

properties.name: resumable
properties.description: True when the runtime supports resuming via `submit({ resume })`.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Opaque payload the app renders. Runtime-specific shape.

name: AgentStateUpdateEvent
description:
Render-state-store sync event. Adapters emit this when the runtime
publishes a state-snapshot intended for the chat library's render store
(used by generative UI and a2ui surfaces).

properties.name: message
properties.description: New user message to append. May be combined with `resume` and/or `state` in the same submit call.

properties.name: resume
properties.description: Resume payload for an active interrupt.

properties.name: state
properties.description: State patch to merge before submitting (runtime-interpreted).

name: AgentWithHistory
description:
Extends Agent with a required `history` signal.

Compositions that need time-travel / checkpoint data (chat-timeline,
chat-debug) take this richer contract. Adapters that cannot supply
history should return plain Agent instead of stubbing an empty array.

properties.name: regenerate
properties.description:
Discards the assistant message at the given index AND all messages after
it, then re-runs the agent against the trimmed conversation tail. The
preceding user message (at index - 1) is preserved and re-submitted as
the agent's input. No new user message is added to the history.

Throws if the message at `index` is not 'assistant' role, or if the
agent is currently loading another response.

properties.name: __licenseEnvHint
properties.description: Test-only env hint override. Not part of the stable API.

properties.name: __licensePublicKey
properties.description:
Test-only public-key override. Defaults to the compile-time embedded
`LICENSE_PUBLIC_KEY`. Not part of the stable API.

properties.name: assistantName
properties.description: Override the default assistant display name (default: "Assistant").

properties.name: avatarLabel
properties.description: Override the default AI avatar label (default: "A").

properties.name: license
properties.description: Signed license token from cacheplane.dev. Optional; omitted in dev.

properties.name: renderRegistry
properties.description: Default render registry for generative UI components.

name: ChatToolCallTemplateContext
description:
Template-context surface available to a per-tool template. The first
argument is the ToolCall itself (let-call); status is exposed as a
named context property (let-status="status").

name: Citation
description:
Provider-agnostic citation entry. Populated by adapters from message
metadata (LangGraph additional_kwargs.citations, ag-ui STATE_DELTA at
/citations/{messageId}). Pandoc-formatted [^id]: ... defs in message
content remain in the markdown AST sidecar and are merged via
CitationsResolverService at render time.

properties.name: extra
properties.description: Provider-specific extras (retrieval score, source type, etc.).

properties.name: id
properties.description: Stable id used to match `[^id]` markers in Pandoc-formatted content.

properties.name: index
properties.description: 1-based display order. Stable per-message.

properties.name: citations
properties.description: Provider-agnostic citation list. Populated by adapters.

properties.name: content
properties.description: Plain text, or a list of structured content blocks.

properties.name: extra
properties.description: Runtime-specific extras; do not rely on shape in portable code.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Optional display/author name.

properties.name: reasoning
properties.description:
Reasoning text emitted by the model before/alongside the visible
response. Populated by adapters from {type:'reasoning'} or
{type:'thinking'} content blocks (LangGraph) or REASONING_MESSAGE_*
events (AG-UI). Always a plain string — provider-specific shape
(encrypted blocks, multi-step summaries) is absorbed by the adapter
and not surfaced here.

properties.name: reasoningDurationMs
properties.description:
Wall-clock duration of the reasoning phase in milliseconds.
Populated by the adapter when both start (first reasoning chunk) and
end (first response-text chunk, or final canonical message) are
known. Undefined when reasoning timing isn't available.

properties.name: toolCallId
properties.description: Present when role === 'tool'.

name: MockAgent
description:
Runtime-neutral contract chat primitives consume.

Implementations are produced by runtime adapters (e.g. a LangGraph or
AG-UI adapter) or by user code for custom backends.

`interrupt` and `subagents` are optional: runtimes that do not support these
concepts should leave them undefined, and primitives that need them check
presence and render a neutral fallback when absent.

Invariant: state lives on signals; `events$` carries only things that are
not derivable from signals.

properties.name: regenerate
properties.description:
Discards the assistant message at the given index AND all messages after
it, then re-runs the agent against the trimmed conversation tail. The
preceding user message (at index - 1) is preserved and re-submitted as
the agent's input. No new user message is added to the history.

Throws if the message at `index` is not 'assistant' role, or if the
agent is currently loading another response.

properties.name: stopCount
properties.description: Count of stop() invocations.

properties.name: submitCalls
properties.description: Captured calls to submit() in order.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Optional human-readable name.

properties.name: toolCallId
properties.description: Tool call ID that spawned this subagent.

properties.name: args
properties.description: Arguments. May be partial while streaming (`status !== 'complete'`).

properties.name: error
properties.description: Optional error payload when status === 'error'.

properties.name: result
properties.description: Present when status === 'complete' or 'error'.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Optional — present when the parent provides it. Drives the pill + default-collapsed logic.

name: A2uiComponentDef
signature: object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object | object

name: AgentEvent
description:
Discriminated union of events flowing on `Agent.events$`.

Invariant: state lives on signals (`messages`, `status`, `toolCalls`,
`state`, `interrupt`, `subagents`, `history`); events on `events$`
carry only things that are not derivable from signals. New variants
are added purely additively when patterns prove necessary.

name: ViewRegistry
description:
A registry of view components available for generative UI rendering.
Plain frozen object mapping view names to Angular component types.
Compose via object spread: `views({ ...base, ...more })`.

name: buildA2uiActionMessage
description:
Builds an A2uiActionMessage from handler params and the current surface.
 The action.context is serialized as v1 DynamicValue-wrapped entries.
signature: buildA2uiActionMessage(params: Record<string, unknown>, surface: A2uiSurface): A2uiActionMessage

name: emitBinding
description: Emits a data model binding event if the prop has a binding path.
signature: emitBinding(emit: object, bindings: Record<string, string> | undefined, prop: string, value: unknown): void

name: formatDuration
description:
Render a millisecond duration as a human-readable label suitable for
the chat-reasoning "Thought for Ns" pill.

- <1 s         → "<1s"
- 1–59 s       → "Ns"     (e.g. "4s")
- ≥60 s        → "Nm Ms"  (e.g. "1m 12s", "60m 0s")

Negative or non-finite inputs collapse to "<1s" so a corrupted timing
map never produces noisy output.

name: getMessageType
description:
Maps a Message to a MessageTemplateType.
Exported as a standalone function so it can be unit-tested without DOM rendering.

name: isAssistantMessage
signature: isAssistantMessage(m: Message): m is Message & { role: "assistant" }

name: isLiteralBoolean
signature: isLiteralBoolean(value: unknown): value is { literalBoolean: boolean }

name: isLiteralNumber
signature: isLiteralNumber(value: unknown): value is { literalNumber: number }

name: isLiteralString
signature: isLiteralString(value: unknown): value is { literalString: string }

name: isPathRef
signature: isPathRef(value: unknown): value is { path: string }

name: isSystemMessage
signature: isSystemMessage(m: Message): m is Message & { role: "system" }

name: isToolMessage
signature: isToolMessage(m: Message): m is Message & { role: "tool" }

name: isUserMessage
signature: isUserMessage(m: Message): m is Message & { role: "user" }

name: messageContent
description:
Extracts a human-readable string from a message's content.

`BaseMessage.content` is `string | MessageContentComplex[]`. Reasoning-
capable models (OpenAI gpt-5/o-series, Anthropic) emit complex arrays of
typed blocks: `{type:'text',text}`, `{type:'reasoning',...}`, tool-use
blocks, etc. We render only the visible text portions and skip anything
else. Stringifying the whole array would dump raw JSON like
`[{"type":"text",...}]` into the chat bubble.

name: renderMarkdown
description:
Renders markdown content to sanitized HTML.
Falls back to plain text with newline->br conversion if `marked` is not installed.

name: statusColor
description:
Returns a CSS style string for a subagent's status badge.
Kept exported for backward compatibility with existing consumers; the
preferred way to style status visually is via the `data-status` attribute
+ CSS selectors (see component styles below).

name: submitMessage
description:
Submits a trimmed message to the agent.
Returns the trimmed string on success, or `null` if the input was empty.
signature: submitMessage(agent: Agent, text: string): string | null

name: toRenderRegistry
description: Converts a ViewRegistry to an AngularRegistry for use with RenderSpecComponent.

name: views
description: Creates a view registry from a name → component map.

name: withoutViews
description: Removes views from a registry by name.

name: withViews
description:
Adds views to a registry without overwriting existing entries.
New keys are added; keys that already exist in `base` are preserved.
signature: withViews(base: ViewRegistry, additions: Record<string, Type<unknown>>): ViewRegistry
